scope.prompt: build a really high quality single html file crossy road game with three.js

runs.label: Khala recorded run (prod 2026-06-22); EXECUTED acceptance on 2026-06-23 => failed 6/6 (verified:false)

executedAcceptance.note: Authoritative north-star artifact (reconstructed from the verified prod SSE stream at /tmp/khala-stream.txt, fingerprint fnv1a32:10cb2792, 53426 bytes, preserved at scripts/khala-demo/artifacts/khala-crossy-road-northstar-run.v1.html) was run through the REAL headless acceptance runner (apps/openagents.com/workers/api/src/inference/acceptance-runner) and verifyKhalaCodeCompletion via scripts/khala-demo/run-executed-acceptance.mjs.

checkDetail.loads_without_errors: FAIL — 1 page error during construction: localStorage access throws in the constructor (the Game ctor reads localStorage before the start-btn listener attaches), consistent with the documented crash-on-load defect; the game never finishes constructing.
checkDetail.play_starts_game: FAIL — PLAY did not start the game (started=undefined; loop never ticked).
checkDetail.forward_input_advances_player: FAIL — forward input did not advance the player (state unreadable).
checkDetail.camera_follow_delta_bounded: FAIL — no bounded camera motion observed (state unreadable).
checkDetail.world_keeps_generating_ahead: FAIL — world rows ahead unknown (state unreadable).
checkDetail.restart_resets_state: FAIL — restart did not reset (state unreadable).

executedAcceptance.honestNote: Two distinct, honest reasons for the red: (1) a real load-time crash (localStorage-in-constructor / DOM-ref ordering) so the game never starts; and (2) the artifact never exposes the runner state contract (__openagentsCrossyRoadState/Start/Restart), so the five state-dependent checks fail honestly rather than producing a false green. An artifact that does not expose the contract cannot be execution-verified — by design. A real `failed` beats a fake `passed`.

externalReportedClaims.label: Sakana Fugu Ultra reported comparison
externalReportedClaims.verdictSummary: Reported faster and cheaper; issues included inverted turn direction, wonky camera, no SFX, and not identical to Crossy Road.

externalReportedClaims.label: Claude Opus 4.8 Ultracode reported comparison
externalReportedClaims.verdictSummary: Reported higher quality; issues included retry loops and wrong restart character position.
